title: clematis integrifolia subsp. ochroleuca
intro: Clematis integrifolia subsp. ochroleuca is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Clematis genus, commonly known as the integrifolia or yellow clematis, characterized by its yellow flowers and lance-shaped leaves. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Clematis integrifolia subsp. ochroleuca include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to support immune function and reduce stress. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
